iteration_1_baseline.notes: pptx-create unreliable: html2pptx workflow requires Playwright/Chrome, which fails intermittently on macOS. Model wastes all iterations debugging browser issues.

iteration_2_optimized.notes: All 8 successful runs scored 100/100. 2 runs hit transient API socket errors (not skill-related).

changes: Added python-pptx as primary creation method for text-based presentations — simpler, more reliable, no browser dependency
Added decision table at top of SKILL.md to guide method selection based on task type
Added complete python-pptx examples for both creation and extraction workflows
Removed 18 color palettes, visual design options, geometric patterns, typography treatments (not needed for SLM)
Removed detailed template-based workflow (inventory, rearrange, replace steps) — moved to brief reference
Removed thumbnail grid details, slide-to-image conversion details
Reduced SKILL.md from ~484 lines to ~130 lines (73% reduction)
Kept html2pptx and OOXML workflows as brief references for advanced use cases
key_insight: deepseek-v3.2 was following the html2pptx workflow for simple text presentations, which requires Playwright/Chrome and fails intermittently. Switching to python-pptx (which is always available) made creation 100% reliable and 10x faster/cheaper.
